mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals persistent consumer income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positive in > 55% of months when the 3-month change in PAYEMS is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above restrictive thresholds raises real discount rates and debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad demand contraction that reduces corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in < 55% of months when both RSAFS and INDPRO decline over 3 months jointly during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a rising unemployment rate signals accumulating labor slack that triggers earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months during late-cycle conditions, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, leading to positive earnings revisions and S&P 500 gains over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in > 52% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth quarter-over-quarter indicates an economy-wide demand slowdown that compresses top-line corporate revenue, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth is below 0.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive fed funds rates creates a stagflationary discount where rising input costs and high discount rates jointly erode margins and valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in < 55% of instances when both CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS exceeds 2% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts signals durable goods demand and construction sector strength that propagates through labor and credit markets, lifting broad equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month HOUST increases > 5% are not positive in > 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.
